_schema.description: Hierarchical taxonomy for a collapsible tree diagram. Root node has 'children'; leaf nodes omit 'children'. Depth 2+ nodes are collapsed on load — click to expand. Maximum recommended depth is 4 for legibility.

fields.name: string — label shown on the node
fields.desc: string — description shown in the tooltip
fields.children: array — child nodes. Omit on leaf nodes.
fields.flag: string (optional) — 'warn' colours node blood-red regardless of depth, for highlighting ethically sensitive capabilities

_schema.replace: Swap names, descriptions, and tree structure to match your hierarchy. Add 'flag': 'warn' to any node you want visually flagged. Keep depth ≤ 4 — beyond that, a treemap or circle packing chart handles scale better.

name: Humanitarian AI
desc: Root taxonomy of AI capabilities deployed across the humanitarian sector.

children.name: Data Collection
children.desc: Technologies for gathering field intelligence in crisis contexts — often the highest-risk stage for privacy and consent.

children.name: Remote Sensing
children.desc: Observation from a distance using satellite, aerial, and ground sensor systems.

children.name: Satellite Analysis
children.desc: Optical and SAR imagery for damage mapping, crop monitoring, and displacement assessment at national scale.

children.name: Drone Surveys
children.desc: UAV deployment into inaccessible or hazardous terrain for post-disaster rapid assessment.

children.name: Environmental Sensors
children.desc: IoT devices measuring flood levels, soil moisture, and air quality in real time.

children.name: Crowdsourcing
children.desc: Aggregating data contributed by field staff, partners, and affected communities themselves.

children.name: Social Media Mining
children.desc: Extracting crisis signals from Twitter, Facebook, WhatsApp, and Telegram during acute events.
children.note: Requires careful bias correction — digital access is unequally distributed.

children.name: Field Reporting Apps
children.desc: Structured mobile tools enabling aid workers to submit standardised observational data from the field.

children.name: Biometric Collection
children.desc: Identity data gathering for beneficiary registration and deduplication.

children.name: Fingerprint ID
children.desc: Biometric matching used widely for beneficiary registration. Less culturally fraught than facial recognition.

children.name: Iris Recognition
children.desc: Higher-accuracy biometric matching. Used by UNHCR in refugee registration contexts.

children.name: Facial Recognition
children.desc: Image-based identity verification. High accuracy; high ethical risk — consent and misuse concerns are severe.

children.name: Analysis & Prediction
children.desc: AI methods that interpret collected data, detect patterns, and forecast future conditions.

children.name: Machine Learning
children.desc: Statistical learning models trained on labelled or unlabelled data to produce predictions and classifications.

children.name: Classification
children.desc: Assigning crisis severity levels, vulnerability tiers, or damage categories to geographic areas or households.

children.name: Regression
children.desc: Predicting continuous outcomes: food prices, displacement volume, disease incidence, or programme costs.

children.name: Clustering
children.desc: Grouping beneficiaries or events into natural segments without predefined categories — used for needs profiling.

children.name: Natural Language Processing
children.desc: Extracting structured meaning from unstructured text across multiple languages and low-resource dialects.

children.name: Machine Translation
children.desc: Enabling communication across language barriers between aid workers and affected communities.

children.name: Named Entity Recognition
children.desc: Extracting locations, organisations, and people from field reports and situation reports at scale.

children.name: Sentiment Analysis
children.desc: Gauging community trust, need urgency, and programme satisfaction from feedback text.

children.name: Computer Vision
children.desc: Automated interpretation of images and video to extract structured information at scale.

children.name: Damage Assessment
children.desc: Classifying building and infrastructure damage levels from aerial imagery post-disaster.

children.name: Population Estimation
children.desc: Counting displaced persons and shelter structures from satellite and drone imagery.

children.name: Medical Imaging AI
children.desc: Diagnosis assistance for malnutrition, injury, and disease from clinical photographs in low-resource settings.

children.name: Decision Support
children.desc: Tools that assist coordinators in making resource, logistics, and response decisions under uncertainty.

children.name: Resource Allocation
children.desc: Optimising the distribution of finite aid resources across competing priorities.

children.name: Logistics Optimisation
children.desc: Routing algorithms for convoy planning, hub placement, and last-mile delivery scheduling.

children.name: Supply Forecasting
children.desc: Predictive models to pre-position supplies before crisis peaks — reducing emergency procurement costs.

children.name: Risk Assessment
children.desc: Identifying where and when crises are most likely to escalate before they occur.

children.name: Vulnerability Mapping
children.desc: Composite scoring of population exposure, adaptive capacity, and structural fragility across regions.

children.name: Early Warning Systems
children.desc: Threshold-triggered alerts for famine, flood, conflict escalation, and epidemic onset.

children.name: Coordination Platforms
children.desc: Shared information systems enabling data exchange and operational collaboration across agencies.

children.name: Information Management
children.desc: Centralised data repositories and standardised inter-agency data sharing protocols (4W, Who Does What Where When).

children.name: Situation Dashboards
children.desc: Real-time operational overviews surfacing key metrics for field and headquarters decision-makers.

children.name: Delivery & Accountability
children.desc: AI supporting the actual distribution of aid and evidence that it reached the right people.

children.name: Beneficiary Services
children.desc: Tools that directly support the registration, targeting, and assistance of affected populations.

children.name: Needs Assessment AI
children.desc: Automated survey analysis to identify and rank individual household needs at scale.

children.name: Cash Transfer Systems
children.desc: Digital disbursement platforms integrating biometrics or mobile wallets for direct assistance.

children.name: Outcome Monitoring
children.desc: Tracking whether aid reaches intended recipients and achieves its stated objectives.

children.name: Distribution Tracking
children.desc: Confirming aid delivery with beneficiary signatures, GPS logs, and digital receipts.

children.name: Impact Measurement
children.desc: Longitudinal data collection and causal inference models assessing sustained programme outcomes.

children.name: Feedback Loops
children.desc: Channels for affected communities to report on aid quality, gaps, and grievances.

children.name: Community Hotlines
children.desc: SMS, voice, and AI chatbot channels for beneficiary queries, complaints, and suggestions.

children.name: Perception Surveys
children.desc: Regular sampling of community satisfaction, trust, and accountability using structured questionnaires.
